STRICT WORKFLOW RULE (CRITICAL): 
- You must remain on the CURRENT branch. Do not switch to or create any other branches.
- STRICT SECURITY RULE: Under NO circumstances are you allowed to modify the 'main' branch or any other existing permanent branches directly. 
- Do not attempt to manually create Pull Requests or push code using external command-line tools.
- YOUR TASK: Simply perform the requested modifications on the CURRENT temporary branch. Stay exactly where you are.

# Project Maintenance & Safety Guidelines
## 🚫 Strictly Forbidden
1. Reject Normalize Scheme: NO `normalized` keyword in `.github/workflows/build-kernel-release.yml`.
2. Reject KSUN/WildKSU: NO `KSUN` or `WildKSU` in `.github/actions/action.yml`.
3. Preserve Telegram Links: Keep `t.me/huangdihd` and `t.me/huangdihd_wildkernel`.
4. Preserve AnyKernel3 Repo: MUST point to `https://github.com/huangdihd/AnyKernel3`.
5. Reject WILD Suffix: `"uname"` MUST be `"OP-RESUKISU"`. NO `-OP-WILD`.
6. Preserve USB DWC3 & OTG Support: DO NOT remove this step in `action.yml`.
7. Preserve Correct ReSukiSU Repository: MUST use `https://github.com/ReSukiSU/ReSukiSU`.
8. Preserve Patch Failure Output: Do NOT modify the output format or logic when a patch fails.
9. Delete Kernel Tracker: The tracking workflow (`oplus-kernel-monitor.yml`) has been automatically deleted. Do NOT attempt to restore it.
10. Release Verification: When checking for releases (e.g., toolchains), you MUST use `gh cli` to verify it is a true release (`gh release view <tag>`), do not simply check if a tag exists.
11. Avoid using  `--fuzz=3` in patch command, which is dangrous.
